item.title: Iran calls deadly attack on Kuwait retaliation for latest U.S. strikes
item.description: After Trump insisted talks with Iran were "going on continuously," Iran fired a deadly missile and drone salvo at Kuwait, claiming retaliation for new U.S. strikes.

item.title: Far-right politicians claim U.K. teen's murder shows "two-tier policing"
item.description: Protesters angry over the murder of Henry Nowak and the way he was treated by officers after being fatally stabbed clashed violently with British police.

item.title: CBS News fires longtime 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley
item.description: CBS News has fired longtime 60 Minutes correspondent and former "CBS Evening News" anchor Scott Pelley one day after he had a tense and confrontational exchange with new 60 Minutes executive producer Nick Bilton.

item.title: Iowa GOP Senate candidate Hinson: Prolonged Iran war could be "political liability"
item.description: Iowa GOP Senate candidate Rep. Ashley Hinson acknowledged the U.S.-Iran conflict could become a "political liability" if it stretches beyond the next few weeks, according to audio obtained by CBS News.

item.title: Amazon faces lawsuit over Ring facial recognition software
item.description: A Virginia man is suing Amazon over Ring's "Familiar Faces" feature, alleging the technology violates people's privacy.

item.title: Tribeca at 25: NYC festival celebrates music, Broadway and New York stories
item.description: The 25th edition of the film and media festival opens on Wednesday, showcasing more than 100 world premiere documentaries and narrative features, as well as short films, live music, podcasts and conversations with leading entertainment figures.

item.title: The Lindbergh Conspiracies | Post Mortem
item.description: "The Lindbergh Conspiracies" host Joe Nocera joins CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod for a discussion about one of the most infamous true crime cases of the 20th century. The 1932 kidnapping of Charles Lindbergh's infant son captivated the world and became one of the first media frenzies of the modern era. But did convicted kidnapper Bruno Hauptmann act alone or was there more to the story? Learn why this case has been the subject of countless conspiracy theories.

item.title: Peabo Bryson, singer known for Disney movie hits, dies at 75
item.description: Peabo Bryson, a two-time Grammy-winning singer and songwriter known for Disney movie hits "Beauty and the Beast" and "A Whole New World," has died at age 75.

item.title: Iowa Senate race set between Democrat Josh Turek, Republican Ashley Hinson
item.description: Republican Sen. Joni Ernst is not running for reelection, and Democrats have been increasingly bullish about their chances of flipping the seat in recent months.

item.title: Gang leader "Black Cat" arrested over 19 killings in Honduras
item.description: The killings took place in the restive Bajo Aguan region of Honduras where rival gangs have fought over control of palm farms and drug trafficking routes.

item.title: Bitter GOP primary for South Dakota governor heads to runoff as Toby Doeden advances
item.description: Businessman and self-described outsider Toby Doeden will advance to a runoff for the GOP nomination for South Dakota governor, CBS News has projected, after a competitive and frequently contentious primary.

item.title: Zach Lahn wins Iowa GOP governor primary, upsetting Trump-backed rival
item.description: MAHA-aligned Republican Zach Lahn will face Democrat Rob Sand in this year's race for Iowa governor, setting up what could be an unusually competitive contest in a red-leaning state.

item.title: Suspect barricaded with hostages in Southern California bank, officials say
item.description: Bakersfield police said the suspect had agreed to release two of the hostages.

item.title: Upcoming billing change could make pregnancy pricier
item.description: Come January, pregnancy care physician billing codes will change from a bundled system to an à la carte one.

item.title: Connie Chan and Scott Wiener advance in race to succeed Pelosi, CBS News projects
item.description: Voters in San Francisco on Tuesday advanced San Francisco Supervisor Connie Chan and state Sen. Scott Wiener in the race to succeed former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi in California's 11th Congressional District, CBS News projects.

item.title: U.S., Iran trade attacks as Iranian officer says renewed war seems "inevitable"
item.description: Prospects for a U.S.-Iran deal appear to be dimming as the war between Israel and Hezbollah grinds on despite Trump saying they agreed to stop fighting.

item.title: Trump admin. floats tariffs on 60 trading partners after forced labor probe
item.description: The Trump administration has unveiled proposed tariffs of 10% or more on dozens of countries accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, including some of the U.S.'s largest trading partners.

item.title: California governor's race set to narrow as voters decide in 2026 primary
item.description: Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer have emerged as the leading contenders to advance to November's general election, according to polling data.

item.title: Mayor Karen Bass will advance to the November election, CBS News projects
item.description: CBS News projects that incumbent Mayor Karen Bass will advance to the November election, while her opponents, Councilmember Nithya Raman and political newcomer Spencer Pratt, compete for the final spot.

item.title: DOJ says it has superseding indictment against Southern Poverty Law Center
item.description: The superseding indictment does not contain any new charges or name new defendants from the original version, which was returned in April.

item.title: Chicago-bound flight diverted to Miami when passenger tries to open door mid-flight
item.description: A former professional MMA fighter from Chicago came to the rescue when an unruly passenger tried to open a Frontier Airlines door mid-flight on Sunday while the plane was headed to O'Hare International Airport.

item.title: Supreme Court lets Alabama use House map that favors GOP in midterms
item.description: The Supreme Court cleared the way for Alabama to use a House map that is more favorable to Republicans, despite a lower court finding that the plan intentionally discriminated against Black voters.

item.title: GOP Rep. Kean returning to in-person work in "matter of weeks" after absence
item.description: The New Jersey congressman last voted on March 5.

item.title: Threat of massive blast eliminated for California chemical leak, officials say
item.description: More than 50,000 people in California remain evacuated due to an Orange County chemical leak that prompted Gov. Newsom to request that President Trump issue a Federal Emergency Declaration.

item.title: Democrat Christina Bohannan to take on Iowa GOP Rep. Miller-Meeks for third time
item.description: Bohannan has lost to the Republican incumbent twice, but narrowed the margin to 799 votes in the 2024 election.

item.title: Lynette Hooker's Bahamas disappearance being investigated as possible murder
item.description: According to an official familiar with the investigation, new digital forensic evidence appears to undercut Brian Hooker's account of his wife's disappearance, although they did not specify how.

item.title: Louisville officer may be fired after killing unarmed suspect
item.description: Warning: Graphic video. A Louisville police officer faced discipline on Tuesday after body camera footage showed him shooting and killing a suspect who was naked and unarmed on Saturday. Jericka Duncan has more details.

item.title: 6/2: CBS Evening News
item.description: Hostage situation, bomb threat reported at California Chase Bank; President Trump names controversial new acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Justice Department charges 4 people in connection with drug-smuggling tunnel connecting U.S., Mexico
item.description: Federal authorities have arrested and charged four people with drug trafficking after a monthslong investigation uncovered what federal officials called a massive, 2,000-foot-long underground tunnel connecting Mexico to the U.S. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has the details.

item.title: Reliving The Nightmare In Tiananmen
item.description: CBS News Cameraman Brian Robbins Was In Beijing On June 4, 1989, as an Uprising was Quashed
